LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT LAO YING'S(AKA: CHRISTY LAO) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHAT PLAINTIFF KNEW AND WHEN PLAINTIFF KNEW IT CONCERNING DEFENDANT'S RESIDENCE AND MAILING ADDRESSES THAT BEAR ON WHETHER PLAINTIFF'S ATTEMPTS AT SERVICE WERE REASONABLE UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT LAO YING'S(AKA: CHRISTY LAO) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. There are triable issues of material fact as to what plaintiff knew and when plaintiff knew it concerning defendant's residence and mailing addresses that bear on whether plaintiff's attempts at service were reasonable under the circumstances. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR#: 8189 (301/PJB/PM)

Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Compel Furtehr Responses Tos Pecial Interrogatories & Requests For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Dec-14-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
John Kithas, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & moving party: Nanyang Commercial Bank
Steven Lau, Esq., appeared for Defendant & opposing party: Lao Ying

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs Nanyang Commercial Bank, Ltd.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents is denied as it is an overly broad invasion of Defendant's right to financial privacy. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
